name: the primitive type called eat

phase_0.boundary_condition: the outermost set boundary defining the scope of the hierarchy

VINIT.justification: the uninitialized state before any elements or subsets are defined

TANCH.justification: the terminal boundary that contains all nested hierarchies

AFWD.justification: the forward morphism that places an element into a container

AREV.justification: the reverse morphism that retrieves an element from its container

CLINK.justification: the composition of inclusion relations where A is in B and B is in C

IMSCRIB.justification: the recognition that a set is a subset of itself

FSPLIT.justification: the division of a set into distinct disjoint subsets

FFUSE.justification: the reconstitution of the original set from its disjoint partitions

EVALT.justification: the affirmative state where the subset is strictly contained within the superset

EVALF.justification: the negative state where the element exists outside the specified container

ENGAGR.justification: the state where an element exists on the boundary, simultaneously inside and outside

IFIX.justification: the permanent fixation of an element's position within the hierarchy

phase_3.void_description: the absence of any topological structure or elements
phase_3.true_description: successful containment within the nested hierarchy

reverse the inclusion attempt for the external element
return to the primary container state
evaluate a boundary condition
hold the state of an element intersecting the boundary
record the boundary intersection event
resolve the topological state
close the universal set boundary
